item: WE-8 - QA: unit-sprite parity gate + bake_unit.py regression instrument
note: run1 executed 2026-07-07 11:11-11:13Z with the ORIGINAL per-run-atlas design: 16/16 in-view composited units pixel-identical (RGBA byte-compare, independent /tiledump?atlas=1 crop at raw lua-read texpos ids vs served /unit-sprite PNG) and 16/16 geometry-identical (sw/sh/ax/ay). Its single FAIL check was a bug in the gate itself, not the exporter: the AUX wire cross-check compared against ALL exported units instead of only units inside the probe player viewport (the wire only ever carries ah for in-view units) -- fixed in this commit (scoped to the window; skips, not fails, when nothing is in view). run1 atlas dump took 104.3s of render-thread saturation and froze the live game -- INCIDENT. The gate was restructured the same hour: atlas is now a persistent cache keyed by save_dir, reused across runs; a fresh dump requires --refresh-atlas --confirmed-with-jt (explicit OK) + DF_LOCK. safety-check/safety-check2 verify the hardened CANNOT-RUN + flag-restore paths live. A first cache-seeded full run is queued for the owner morning approval; the permission system independently blocked an agent-initiated seed attempt at 12:3xZ, confirming the gate's own policy.

checks.name: 100% geometry match (sw/sh/ax/ay, independent texpos read vs shipping listing)
100% pixel match (RGBA byte-identical, independent atlas crop vs served PNG)

wire_check.reason: no exported unit falls inside player 'qa-unitsprites''s current interest window (cam={'player': 'qa-unitsprites', 'x': 70, 'y': 105, 'z': 165, 'zoom': 100, 'zoomExplicit': False}, w=50, h=28) -- not a failure, just nothing in view to cross-check

safety_check_runs.label: safety-check2

wire_check.reason: no exported unit falls inside player 'qa-unitsprites''s current interest window (cam={'player': 'qa-unitsprites', 'x': 70, 'y': 85, 'z': 160, 'zoom': 100, 'zoomExplicit': False}, w=50, h=28) -- not a failure, just nothing in view to cross-check

safety_check_runs.cannot_run_reason: no cached atlas at <DF_ROOT>\dwf_unitsprites_gate\atlas_cache\atlas and --refresh-atlas not given. A fresh dump needs DF_LOCK + --refresh-atlas --confirmed-with-jt (see the safety-rule comment above this block) -- not something this gate does on its own initiative.

bake_unit_baseline.note: gate runs it non-gating against up to 2 live dwarf fixtures per run; run1 predates the integration, first numbers will land with the cache-seeded run
